creator: eugen@freebsd.org
text:
install(1) manual page tells that the install utility can create missing parent directories as required if -d options is specified. This mode seems to work half-way only, as it really creates needed directories but fails to copy specified files there:

$ cd /tmp
$ touch file1 file2
$ install -d dir file1 file2
install: file1 exists but is not a directory
$ ls -lR dir
total 0
